The user has to click into the page again to refocus the app. If you think it's an issue, please file a separate issue (it's off topic for this issue).

The difference in keyboard layout vs locale shouldn't be the issue (since the Portuguese has a and b in the same place as US English).

@gspencergoog I tested your example, and using the Focus widget to capture keyboard events works just as well as the FocusableActionDetector. Thanks a lot for your help!

@fernandooliiveira But wasn't the `FocusableActionDetector` not working for you?  Are you saying that both `Focus` and the `FocusableActionDetector` now work for you? Or just `Focus`?  (it would be odd if one worked and the other didn't considering that the `FocusableActionDetector` uses `Focus` to do its work).

@gspencergoog The `FocusableActionDetector` dont trigger events on Linux. From my tests, it seems like something changed in version 3.19.0. But `Focus` or `FocusScope` are working perfectly, and I can capture all the events on Linux/Windows.

Okay, that is odd. I can successfully run your original code snippet on Linux (as I said above). If `Focus` and `HardwareKeyboard` both work, then it doesn't have to do with the key event system, which is what I would suspect in a case where it works on some versions of Linux and not others. The rest of the code in `FocusableActionDetector` has to do with actions and shortcuts, and should be entirely platform (and especially platform version) independent. The only thing that *might* be affecting things is that in the `FocusableActionDetector`, it wraps the Focus with a `MouseRegion`.

I'm going to leave this open to see if others have a similar problem so that we can collect more data to start to narrow it down, but I don't know what else I can do from here to proceed.

issues.title: [web] TextField doesn't accept input after it is submitted even though it retains focus
comments:
Not only ctrl+a is affected. Also just entering text is bugged.

### Steps to Reproduce
1. Execute flutter run -d chrome on the above code sample
1. Type "foo" into the text field.
1. Hit enter to submit the field.
1. Type "bar".

     
**Expected results:** The textfield should contain "foobar".

**Actual results:** The textfield only contains "foo", but the text field is still focused (the cursor is visible) so it should have added "bar" as well.

Hi @jld3103, thanks for filing the issue. 

Please provide the output of `flutter doctor -v` thank you. 

@GeertJohan, please create a new issue for your scenario and provide the necessary information so that it may be better addressed. Feel free to tag me in it

Thank you.

@danagbemava-nc It is exactly the same scenario. It's just that I didn't describe the text editing issue completely. Basically: Although the text field is focused, keyboard actions are not working (e.g. text selection or appending characters).

issues.title: [Windows] On screen keyboard pops up abnormally on touch screen tablets
issues.body:
screen keyboard pops up abnormally on the Surface tablet

It will not pop up for the first time. You need to manually click the keyboard icon on the taskbar. After that, any click on the position on the software will evoke the on-screen keyboard.

Sample demo video: [video](https://github.com/yh4922/flutter_keyboard_test/raw/main/assets/1.mp4)

Sample code repository: https://github.com/yh4922/flutter_keyboard_test

Hardware platform
```
Processor	Intel(R) Core(TM) i5-8350U CPU @ 1.70GHz   1.90 GHz
Installed RAM	8.00 GB
System type	64-bit operating system, x64-based processor
Pen and touch	Pen and touch support with 10 touch points
```
comments:
When you click button, you need to unfocus from textfield
or 
you can use below code in button click
FocusScope.of(context).requestFocus(FocusNode());

> When you click button, you need to unfocus from textfield or you can use below code in button click FocusScope.of(context).requestFocus(FocusNode());

I modified my code, but it doesn't work, and the onscreen keyboard doesn't hide when I use unfoucs, I think it might be an issue with the flutter engine

@selvam920 
I also found a phenomenon, when I call methods such as `showDialog` or `Navigator`, any click on the screen will pop up the on-screen keyboard

If the user types in the text fields that trigger the keyboard and uses the keyboard enter key to change the focus then the keyboard would retract when the focus ends on an ElevatedButton.

Here is a solution that I am currently using for the release version of the app:


**1. First created an EmptyButton Widget**

```
class EmptyButton extends StatelessWidget {
  final FocusNode? focusNode;

  const EmptyButton({
    Key? key,
    this.focusNode,
  }) : super(key: key);

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    logger("__EMPTY_BUTTON__");
    return ElevatedButton(
        focusNode: focusNode,
        onPressed: () {},
        autofocus: true,
        style: ElevatedButton.styleFrom(
            fixedSize: Size.zero,
            backgroundColor: Colors.transparent,
            shadowColor: Colors.transparent,
            surfaceTintColor: Colors.transparent),
        child: const SizedBox(height: 0));
  }
}

```

**2. Now go to the page and add this button with invisible size like this:**

```
 SizedBox(
                  height: 1.h,
                  width: .5.w,
                  child: const EmptyButton()),

```

This issue is because the popup keyboard remains in progress even when the page changes or the dialog is re-launched.
So if there is a widget that has focus when the context changes by changing the page or launching the dialog, it will be resolved.

If there is no focus widget, you can add the code below.

/// 가상 터치 키보드에서 edit시에 임의로 키보드가 계속 뜨는 오류를 수정해 준다.
Widget get touchKeyboardErrorFix =>
    SizedBox(
      width: 1,
      height: 1,
      child: TextButton(
        autofocus: true,
        onPressed: () {},
        child: const Text(""),
      ),
    );

Your situation may be different, so applying this may not solve the problem, but you can try.

issues.title: Flutter web textfield on iOS: cursor goes to completely wrong place after tap on text
issues.body:
Keep in mind - one can't edit text if you can't place the cursor at least very nearby an intended location on tap.

As run in chrome and safari browsers on android and ios cell phones, after many experiments including a simple flutter dev cookbook case we ask...

seen in flutter version 3.2.2 stable and  3.7.10 stable on 3 people's environments.

issue is also seen using http-server against release flutter build web (build/web) content.

To demonstrate:

Open this url  **on a cell phone**
https://docs.flutter.dev/cookbook/forms/focus

1 in the cookbook dev code editor add:
   maxLines: 5

2 click run 

3  in the textfield, paste the ~1500 char's "lorem ipsum" text below.  

4 tap various places within the text (once the on screen keyboard settles) and 

5 !!!!!!  issue: you will see cursor does not end up where the  tap occurred. bounces to other places, with underlines and confused attempts at hi-lighting I guess

6 this is just one environment to demo this issue in.  I've tried many different tests.  (I'd be happy to see even one TextField example where this does not occur, on a cell phone.)

```

issues.title: [iOS] TextField gets hidden by the keyboard due to out of sync animation
issues.body:
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Steps to reproduce

This issue has been reproduced on old iphone models (tested on iphone 7 with iOS 15.7.9)

Sample code here:
https://github.com/delfme/flutter-native-text-input-repo/tree/3ef4459c0aad47b8d74d90340096e27844a18b9f




### Expected results

Bottom inset should move in sync with keyboard native animation.

### Actual results

There is a 4s animation delay, and during this time textfield is covered by the keyboard, see video from this comment (tested on iPhone 7, iOS 15.7.9) https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/138845#issuecomment-1825131443



### Code sample

Sample here: https://github.com/delfme/flutter-native-text-input-repo/tree/3ef4459c0aad47b8d74d90340096e27844a18b9f

Do you notice this issue on your another iOS physical device?

With iphone 13 pro iOS 16.3.1, Im sometimes hit by a shake on the first opening, then it is smooth. Probably it is that janky initial frame.

Can someone make time for this and the android equivalent?
I do understand it is not easy, but we cannot show to users a shaking textfield.

Checking this on Android, there's not much jank either but raster can be easily seen when the keyboard pops up. At that time, the input chat view (on the keyboard) will have a slight jerking effect. And I think it could be similar to https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/120435. 

Checking again on iOS, I indeed no longer see the original issue (4s animation delay). However, sometimes a slight jerking effect on the chat view input still exists when the keyboard pops up; no jank at that time, and it is much less raster than Android (mostly just UI frames). I will label the issue for other's input to see if there's a way to explore this more clearly.

issues.title: Keyboard pops up automatically after app goes to background and comes back
issues.body:
## Steps to Reproduce I was able to reproduce in two scenarios 1. Go to https://wen-el-sahra.web.app/destination/Sunset . Use the search feature and search for The Broad. Click on book now, now minimise the browser and come back again. The keyboard will start to appear. After the keyboard appears like this, it will keep on automatically popping up on navigating to new routes or popping and going back to previous ones. This issue is only happening if there are buttons before a form, otherwise this is not happening. To test the above theory go to https://wen-el-sahra.web.app/destination/BirthdayBachelor and select the first card named se and click book now and perform the above steps the keyboard won't appear. 2. Go to https://wen-el-sahra.web.app/destination/Sunset use the search feature and search for something. Minimise and come back to the app and the keyboard will appear automatically. This behaviour may be device specific as previously a [similar issue](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/67341) was solved and closed in flutter framework that was device specific. Below are the specifications of the test device. I am testing on a real android device. **Expected results:** Keyboard should not auto pop up on app coming back from background on android **Actual results:** Keyboard automatically pops up on app coming back from background on android

> 2. User picks entry to autofill

> Session with autofill

facing the same issue guys..looking for a workaround right now. This also needs to be looked at,thanks.

> If you are using any OnBoarding Functionality And or using GETX So when you move from 1 page to another where you no longer require on that page use: Example::
> 
> Get.offAll(const LoginScreen())?.then((value) async { Get.delete(); });
> 
> This has solve mine. Hope it helps someone.

this workaround doesnt work when you need the previous page. but is fine if you dont need it,thanks.

Could this be a problem with how we're handling/producing CR/LF pairs? Perhaps we should investigate whether the embedder on Windows needs CRs inserted, and how we calculate text ranges? (Desktop Triage)
cc @loic-sharma @yaakovschectman

I am not sure _why_ this is happening, but I have investigated and found that, at least I think, the cursor in the text selection actually is on the newline in these bugged cases, but is rendered at the start of the text field instead of its actual position. If you click in the newline space where it reproduces the bug, and then try to type, the new text appears in the correct space. So really, I think this is a bug with where we render the cursor on screen, not where we place it within the text.

Additionally, when pasting the test text into Notepad++ before pasting directly into Flutter, then copying out of Notepad++ into Flutter, optionally changing newline encoding first, the bug is only reproduced when the text is set to Windows-style newlines (CRLF). When using Unix-style (LF), the cursor is rendered in the correct space.

@justinmc When I click on the empty line, the cursor is rendered at the start of the text instead of at the empty line. However, when I start typing, the new text appears at the empty where it should, and as soon as one character is added, the cursor moves in front of that character, as would be expected. Also, when navigating using the arrow keys, moving forwards or back moves the cursor to where it should be if it were on the empty line beforehand. The behavior regarding typing and navigating is exactly what I would expect it to be when working properly, but the cursor is rendered at the start of the text when I would expect it to be on the empty line. The bug appears to be only visual.
The `triaged-desktop` label is irrelevant if there is no `team-desktop` label or `fyi-desktop` label.

- Clone the flutter repo 
- Compile the flutter engine for android
- Build the flutter engine for android 
- Run the android tests (`./testing/run-tests.py --type=java)

The test passes and it doesn't leak any resources

### Actual results

N/A

N/A

Hello @zhu-flyingsheep. Thank you for filing this issue. Can you please provide the following information?

- A [complete and minimal, reproducible example](https://stackoverflow.com/help/minimal-reproducible-example).

Please provide the information in the form of text. See how to make collapsible sections with Markdown [here](https://docs.github.com/en/get-started/writing-on-github/working-with-advanced-formatting/organizing-information-with-collapsed-sections).

When I click on the widget TextField of flutter, and then click the input text of the webview, two cursors will appear on the screen. TextField doesn't seem to lose focus，At this point, click the input text of the webview, and the keyboard will still appear above the TextField widget. keyboard will hide the input text of webview @exaby73

As per your original post, you are describing an entirely different bug, which doesn't seem to be reproducible. Are you raising a bug with the keyboard hiding the input field (which is not reproducible) or the dual cursors (which is reproducible)?

In either case, this issue is invalid. The dual cursors is not something Flutter could realistically handle since Flutter does not control the focus of he webview but only it's own focus. You can handle this by detecting if Flutter has gotten the focus back through a `FocusNode` on the `TextField` and run some JavaScript using the `WebViewController` to blur the input field there.

I am closing this issue for those reasons. If you disagree with my decision, please comment and I can reopen it. Thank you.

import 'package:flutter/material.dart';
import 'package:two_dimensional_scrollables/two_dimensional_scrollables.dart';

void main() {
  runApp(const MainApp());
}

class MainApp extends StatefulWidget {
  const MainApp({super.key});

  @override
  State<MainApp> createState() => _MainAppState();
}

class _MainAppState extends State<MainApp> {
  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return MaterialApp(home: HomeScreen());
  }
}

class HomeScreen extends StatefulWidget {
  const HomeScreen({super.key});

  @override
  State<HomeScreen> createState() => _HomeScreenState();
}

class _HomeScreenState extends State<HomeScreen> {
  final _verticalScrollController = ScrollController();
  final _horizontalScrollController = ScrollController();

  @override
  void dispose() {
    _verticalScrollController.dispose();
    _horizontalScrollController.dispose();
    super.dispose();
  }

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return Scaffold(
      appBar: AppBar(title: Text('Home')),
      body: SelectionArea(
        child: CustomScrollView(
          slivers: [
            ...List.generate(10, (index) {
              return SliverToBoxAdapter(
                child: Container(
                  padding: .all(16),
                  child: Text(index.toString()),
                ),
              );
            }),
            SliverToBoxAdapter(
              child: SizedBox(
                height: 300,
                child: TableView.builder(
                  rowCount: 100,
                  columnCount: 100,
                  verticalDetails: ScrollableDetails.vertical(
                    controller: _verticalScrollController,
                  ),
                  horizontalDetails: ScrollableDetails.horizontal(
                    controller: _horizontalScrollController,
                  ),
                  columnBuilder: (column) {
                    return TableSpan(extent: FixedSpanExtent(100));
                  },
                  rowBuilder: (row) {
                    return TableSpan(extent: FixedSpanExtent(100));
                  },
                  cellBuilder: (context, vicinity) {
                    return TableViewCell(
                      child: Container(
                        alignment: Alignment.center,
                        color: vicinity.row.isOdd
                            ? vicinity.column.isEven
                                  ? Colors.blue.shade300
                                  : Colors.yellow.shade300
                            : vicinity.column.isOdd
                            ? Colors.blue.shade300
                            : Colors.yellow.shade300,
                        child: Text('(${vicinity.column},${vicinity.row})'),
                      ),
                    );
                  },
                ),
              ),
            ),
            ...List.generate(10, (index) {
              return SliverToBoxAdapter(
                child: Container(
                  padding: .all(16),
                  child: Text(index.toString()),
                ),
              );
            }),
          ],
        ),
      ),
    );
  }
}
